title: Your First Ansible Ad-Hoc Command
description: Write an Ansible ad-hoc style playbook that pings localhost and gathers facts using Fully Qualified Collection Names (FQCN).
starterCode:
---
# Complete this playbook
# Use FQCN for all modules (e.g., ansible.builtin.ping)
- name: My first Ansible playbook
  hosts: localhost
  # Add connection type here
  tasks:
    - name: Ping the control node
      # Add the ping module here

    - name: Print a message
      # Use ansible.builtin.debug to print 'Hello from Ansible 2.19!'
solutionCode:
---
- name: My first Ansible playbook
  hosts: localhost
  connection: local
  tasks:
    - name: Ping the control node
      ansible.builtin.ping:

    - name: Print a message
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: 'Hello from Ansible 2.19!'
hints: Use 'connection: local' when targeting localhost to avoid SSH
The FQCN for ping is ansible.builtin.ping (not just ping)
ansible.builtin.debug uses the 'msg' parameter to print text

validationRules.errorMessage: Add 'connection: local' under the play to avoid SSH to localhost
Add an ansible.builtin.debug task to print a message
Your YAML is not valid — check indentation and syntax
